medium_risk_samples.desc: The road between Bhit CP-4 and CP-5 security check posts has been damaged by recent rainfall, which has caused the sand base to wash away. This poses a significant hazard to the maintenance teams who travel daily to the

Observed one production employee dealing with MCC starter after tripped of HVAC and was pulling the MCC starter outside to check and reset the circuit, that is purely the domain of Electrical Team and may cause flashover

Bhit wellhead Road’s survey was carried out after heavy rain spell:
CP-4 to CP-5 Road: 
•	Pipe culverts (drain pipe) have been blocked due to land slide, preventing proper drainage and leading to water accumulation benea
Hazards related to night activities and excessive hot temperatures were not included in the risk assessment issued for scaling removal activity for after-cooler fans.
The emergency exit gate at Bhit-2, located near the produced water pit, was found open. The lock and key were also missing.
During drill at propane storage area, it has been observed that 2 fire water hydrants are already engaged due to ongoing water showering activity on after cooler fans due to operational constraints.

The road between Bhit CP-4 and CP-5 security check posts has been damaged by recent rain, causing the sand base to wash away. This poses a significant hazard to the maintenance teams who travel daily to the Bhit site. Fr

During the after-cooler cleaning activity, it was observed that a contractor person was standing by keeping both feet on the top of scaffolding pipes to reach the targeted area, creating an unsafe situation. where slippe
During the D-9 road repair activity, the local labor deputed for this job were observed wearing damaged safety shoes, specifically with compromised soles.

During the overhaul of the instrument air compressor B it was confirmed that energy isolation measures were in place and effective. However it was noted that the isolation tag on the process isolation valve was missing.
During re-inspection of contractor dumpers, pressure brake leakages was observed in one dumper, and another was also unable to perform its function effectively
During lifting activity with boom Truck to load the old STP stair on Hino Truck, Area was not barricaded and two unauthorized people without PPEs compliance holding the stair

During a site visit at D-9 road repair work, it was observed that 4–5 contractor staff were attempting to manually lift a large metallic drum filled with water in order to pour the water into a wheel loader bucket. The d
Coaster driver was sleeping or taking rest on side of D-09 road entrance by keeping sheet underneath with bare feet posing snake bite hazard and also possible vehicle hit hazard

Fumigation was carried out at Camp-1 accommodation rooms by the TFS fumigator. However, an unbearable smell was later reported by several room residents. It was found that the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for the fumigation c

recommendations.action: PPE checkpoint at plant entry; toolbox talks on eye protection and footwear in camp/workshop areas.
Valve isolation tagging (DO NOT OPERATE); line-up verification; amine mothballing compliance.
100% tie-off above 1.8m; barricades for overhead work; SWA audit for contractors.
Weekly housekeeping audits Camp-1/Camp-2; cable/hose routing in plant areas.
Road repair CP-4/CP-5; speed enforcement; vehicle permit compliance at wellheads.
Camp HSE walkdowns; mess hall safety; illumination upgrades.
Process safety inspections; amine drainage containment; SIMOPS review.
Monthly open-item review; escalate overdue items to department heads.

insights: Sheet 1 — UA/UC: 1621 reports, 91.3% closed, 138 open (88 overdue).
Sheet 2 — Good Obs: 1509 positive reports (93% of UA/UC volume).
Top UA/UC dept: Bhit HSE (518); top Good Obs dept: Bhit Production.
